0101.title: DCP APPOINTED IN WRITING

quotes.text: Establish a Directives Control Point (DCP) to manage and administer the command directives management program. CMC (ARDB) will serve as the DCP for Headquarters directives. Field commands are required to designate a DCP in writing.

quotes.citation: MCO 5215.1K w/Admin CH-3, Chapter 1, paragraph 8 (Directives Control Point (DCP))
quotes.text: HQMC staff agencies and field commanders shall establish a DCP to comply with the responsibilities and procedures detailed for processing directives. Field commands are required to designate a DCP in writing. CMC (ARDB) is the DCP for HQMC directives.

regulatory.summary: The command must designate a DCP in writing to manage and administer the command directives management program.

actions: Ask the command to produce the written appointment of the DCP
The appointment must be in writing, not verbal
Letter signed by the commanding officer or principal official
Identifies the appointee by name and rank or grade
Identifies the appointee by billet or position
States the DCP responsibilities
Dated within the current command tenure
Cross-check appointee against current command roster
howToInspect.callout: If new appointee not yet designated after departure

actions: Interview the appointed DCP
Ask the DCP to describe their responsibilities
Ask the DCP to demonstrate their understanding of directives management procedures
Ask the DCP to identify location of master directive file
Letter maintained in the directives management program file
Letter readily accessible upon request
Copy provided to the DCP appointee
No written appointment letter on file
Verbal designation only
Letter signed by appointee instead of commanding officer
Appointee no longer assigned to the command
DCP appointee unaware of duties
Letter undated or missing key elements

questionsToAsk: Show me your written appointment letter for the Directives Control Point?
Who is your current DCP and when were they appointed?
What are the DCP responsibilities under MCO 5215.1K?
Walk me through how you manage the command directives management program?
Where is the master directive file maintained?
verification: Written appointment letter on file
Letter signed by commanding officer or principal official
Appointee identified by name and billet
DCP responsibilities stated in the letter
Letter dated within current command tenure
Appointee currently assigned to the command
DCP demonstrates knowledge of duties
Letter filed in directives management program file
Letter readily accessible for review

0102.title: DIRECTIVES MANAGEMENT PROGRAM ESTABLISHED

quotes.citation: MCO 5215.1K w/Admin CH-3, paragraph 4.a.(1)(b)2 (Designation of Directives)
quotes.text: Field commands shall issue local directives in the name of the Commander using the designation line of the command issuing the directive; e.g., I Marine Expeditionary Force (MEF) Order (IMEFO) or I Marine Expeditionary Force (MEF) Bulletin (IMEFBul).
Sponsors will ensure directives under their purview comply with this Order.

Establish a Directives Control Point (DCP) to manage and administer the command directives management program. CMC (ARDB) will serve as the DCP for Headquarters directives. Field commands are required to designate a DCP in writing.

regulatory.summary: The command must establish a Directives Management Program through a local policy directive or written procedures that outline the staffing process and review cycle of directives, ensuring local directives comply with MCO 5215.1K and are issued in the name of the Commander.

howToInspect.step: Request the Directives Management Program Documentation
actions: Ask the command to produce the local policy directive establishing the program
Or written procedures outlining the staffing and review process
The program documentation must address:
Staffing process for new directives
Review cycle for existing directives
Roles and responsibilities of the DCP
Format requirements per MCO 5215.1K
Approval and signature authority
Filing and retention procedures
Reference the template directive on the ARDB public website
URL: https://www.ar.marines.mil/Branches/Publishing-and-Logistics-Management/Records-Reports-Directives-and-Forms/
Verify command program addresses the same key elements

howToInspect.step: Verify Local Directives Are in Commander's Name
actions: Pull a sample of 5 to 10 local directives
Verify each is issued in the name of the Commander
Verify designation line uses command identifier (e.g., BnO, RegtO, MEFO)
Sponsors must ensure directives under their purview comply with MCO 5215.1K
Spot check directives for format compliance
Verify the program includes a compliance check before signature
Interview the DCP and any directive sponsors
Ask them to describe the staffing process
Ask them to describe the review cycle
Verify they reference the local program documentation
No local policy directive or written procedures established
Program documentation does not address staffing process
Program documentation does not address review cycle
Local directives not issued in name of the Commander
Sponsors unaware of MCO 5215.1K compliance requirements
Program exists in practice but not documented

questionsToAsk: Show me your local policy directive establishing the Directives Management Program?
Walk me through your staffing process for a new directive?
What is your review cycle for existing directives?
Pull a recent local directive and show me how it complies with MCO 5215.1K?
Who is responsible for ensuring directives comply with the parent order?
verification: Local policy directive or written procedures established
Staffing process documented
Review cycle documented
DCP roles and responsibilities defined
Local directives issued in name of Commander
Designation line uses correct command identifier
Sponsors demonstrate compliance knowledge
Program documentation readily accessible

0103.title: DIRECTIVES OVER 9 YEARS WITHOUT REISSUANCE

quotes.citation: MCO 5215.1K w/Admin CH-3, paragraph 4.b.(2)(d)2 (Conduct a Directives Review)
quotes.text: Revise an order when it is 9 years old; do not republish just for that reason.
Justify and document your records when a directive cannot be updated or cancelled; monitor target dates for completion or cancellation.

regulatory.summary: The command must revise any order reaching its 9-year anniversary. Substantial or administrative changes do not restart or extend the 9-year lifecycle. Orders that cannot be revised by the 9-year mark may be certified as current for one additional year by the signing authority, with the justification documented.

actions: Request the command's complete listing of active directives
Listing must include original signature date for each directive
Include all orders (not bulletins, those are covered in Q0104)
For each directive, calculate years since original signature date
Substantial changes do NOT reset the clock
Administrative changes do NOT reset the clock
Only a full revision resets the 9-year lifecycle
Identify all directives at or past the 9-year mark
List all directives 9 years old or older
For each, determine status: revised, certified current, or expired
If a directive is certified current beyond 9 years, request the certification documentation
Certification must be by the signing authority
Certification valid only for 1 additional year
If a directive cannot be revised or cancelled, justification must be documented
Records must monitor target dates for completion or cancellation
Pull the annual review records (NAVMC 10974) for aged directives
Verify the annual review identified the 9-year status
Verify the review documented the planned action
Directives past 9 years with no revision and no certification
Certifications of currency exceeding 1 additional year
Substantial changes treated as resetting the 9-year clock
No justification documented for directives that cannot be revised
Annual review failed to flag aged directives

questionsToAsk: Show me your master directive listing with original signature dates?
Which of your directives are 9 years old or older?
For this aged directive, show me the revision, certification of currency, or justification?
Walk me through how you track directive age each year?
How do you document a certification of currency by the signing authority?
verification: Master directive listing with original signature dates available
All directives evaluated for 9-year anniversary
No aged directives without documented action
Certifications of currency limited to 1 additional year
Certifications signed by the signing authority
Justification documented when revision not feasible
Annual review process identifies aged directives
Substantial changes not used to reset lifecycle

0104.title: BULLETINS OVER 12 MONTHS OLD

quotes.text: A bulletin is a directive of a one-time or brief term, has the same force and effect as an order, may require a one-time report, and must have a self-canceling provision. It normally remains in effect up to 12 months, but no longer. Action required by a bulletin; e.g., submission of reports, use of forms, maintaining and disposing of records, or following a specific procedure, is cancelled when the bulletin cancels, unless the requirement is incorporated in another directive. If a bulletin with a given cancellation date has cancelled and the material is still required, reissue the material in another directive. Bulletins shall be written in the format prescribed in Chapter 3 of this Order.

regulatory.summary: Bulletins normally remain in effect up to 12 months, but no longer. Bulletins must have a self-canceling provision. If material from a cancelled bulletin is still required, it must be reissued in another directive.

actions: Request the command's complete listing of active bulletins
Listing must include the issue date and self-cancellation date for each bulletin
Each bulletin must have a self-canceling provision
Cancellation date must be visible on the bulletin
For each bulletin, calculate months since issue date
Identify any bulletin in effect more than 12 months
For bulletins that have cancelled, verify if action was still required
If material was still required, verify it was reissued in another directive
howToInspect.callout: Cancelled bulletin material still in use without reissuance

actions: Bulletins must be written in format prescribed in Chapter 3 of MCO 5215.1K
Spot check format compliance
Ask staff sections if any bulletins are still being followed
Verify those bulletins are within the 12-month window
Identify any expired bulletins still in active use
Bulletins in effect more than 12 months
Bulletins without self-canceling provisions
Cancelled bulletin material still being followed without reissuance
Bulletins not in Chapter 3 format
No tracking system for bulletin expiration

questionsToAsk: Show me your active bulletin listing with issue dates and cancellation dates?
Which bulletins are currently in effect?
For this bulletin, show me the self-cancellation provision?
When this bulletin cancelled, where was the required material reissued?
How do you track when bulletins are about to expire?
verification: Active bulletin listing with dates available
Each bulletin has a self-canceling provision
No bulletins in effect more than 12 months
Cancelled bulletin material reissued in other directives if still required
Bulletins formatted per Chapter 3 of MCO 5215.1K
Tracking system in place for bulletin expiration

0105.title: CORRECT FORMAT, LETTERHEAD, AND SMEAC PARAGRAPHS

quotes.citation: MCO 5215.1K w/Admin CH-3, Chapter 1, paragraph 18.a.(2)(a) (Field Commands Stationery)
quotes.text: Use the official command or unit letterhead stationery for the first page. If none is available, type the unit's letterhead in all upper case letters beginning 5/8 of an inch from the top edge of the paper and center all lines.
This paragraph describes the purpose, and must be the first paragraph of the directive.
This is always the second paragraph, if needed. Show the directives being canceled; show the SSIC of a bulletin and include the date of the 'basic' bulletin. Only cancel directives you sponsor.

This paragraph describes the task to be accomplished; clear, concise statements of tasks. When cancellation of an MCO is required, a 'Cancellation.' paragraph will be paragraph '2', and the 'Mission.' paragraph will be paragraph '3'. A cancellation paragraph is required when canceling other directives. When the 'Cancellation' and 'Mission' paragraphs are both used, the directive will have 6 paragraphs.

This paragraph contains clear and concise statements of the commander's intent to implement the directive to accomplish the mission. Amplifies paragraph 2, 'Mission.' The paragraph breakdowns into the following subparagraphs: (1) Commander's Intent and Concept of Operations (a) Commander's Intent (b) Concept of Operations (2) Subordinate Element Missions. This paragraph describes the main effort, supporting efforts, and Reserve. (3) Coordinating Instructions. This paragraph identifies and discusses instructions that are common to two or more elements, coordinating details.
This paragraph describes logistics, specific responsibilities, and support.

This paragraph breaks down into the following subparagraphs: a. Command. This paragraph provides the applicability statement; e.g., 'This Order is applicable to the Marine Corps Total Force' or 'This Order is applicable to the Marine Corps Reserve.' b. Signal. This Order is effective the date signed.

regulatory.summary: Per Chapter 2, paragraph 6 (Signature Block) and paragraph 7 (DISTRIBUTION and Copy to Sections) prescribe the closing format requirements. Command directives must use official command letterhead and follow the SMEAC five-paragraph format: Situation, Mission, Execution, Administration and Logistics, and Command and Signal. When a Cancellation paragraph is needed, it becomes paragraph 2 and the directive has six paragraphs.

howToInspect.step: Pull a Sample of Local Directives
actions: Request 5 to 10 recent local orders and bulletins
Include a mix of directive types and sponsors
For each directive, check the letterhead:
Official command or unit letterhead used
If no official letterhead available, unit letterhead typed in all upper case
Letterhead begins 5/8 inch from top edge of paper
All lines centered
howToInspect.callout: Directive not on official letterhead or improperly typed

actions: For each directive, verify the five mandatory paragraphs:
Paragraph 1: Situation (describes purpose, always first)
Paragraph 2: Mission (or Cancellation if needed)
Paragraph 3: Execution (or Mission if Cancellation is paragraph 2)
Paragraph 4: Administration and Logistics
Paragraph 5: Command and Signal
Six paragraphs total when Cancellation paragraph is used
howToInspect.callout: SMEAC paragraphs out of correct order

actions: Execution paragraph must contain:
(1) Commander's Intent and Concept of Operations
(a) Commander's Intent
(b) Concept of Operations
(2) Subordinate Element Missions
(3) Coordinating Instructions
a. Command - applicability statement present
b. Signal - effective date statement present
howToInspect.callout: Command and Signal missing applicability or effective date

actions: If used, must be paragraph 2
Shows directives being cancelled
Shows SSIC and date of basic bulletins being cancelled
Only cancels directives the sponsor owns
howToInspect.callout: Cancellation cancels directives outside sponsor's ownership

actions: Signature block per Chapter 2 paragraph 6
DISTRIBUTION and Copy to sections per Chapter 2 paragraph 7
howToInspect.callout: Signature block or distribution sections do not follow Chapter 2 par 6/7

actions: Directives missing one or more SMEAC paragraphs
Paragraphs out of correct order
Cancellation paragraph not in position 2 when used
Execution paragraph missing subparagraphs
Command and Signal missing applicability or effective date
Letterhead not on official stationery and not properly typed
Sponsor cancelling directives not under their authority

questionsToAsk: Pull a recent local order and walk me through the SMEAC paragraphs?
Show me where the Commander's Intent appears in this directive?
Why is this paragraph numbered as paragraph 3 instead of 2?
What letterhead does your command use for directives?
Show me the Command and Signal paragraph for this directive?
verification: Sample of 5 to 10 local directives reviewed
Official command letterhead used or properly typed
All five SMEAC paragraphs present
Paragraph order correct
Cancellation paragraph in position 2 when used
Execution paragraph contains all subparagraphs
Command and Signal contains applicability and effective date
Signature block formatted per Chapter 2 paragraph 6
DISTRIBUTION and Copy to sections per Chapter 2 paragraph 7

0106.title: ANNUAL REVIEW VIA NAVMC 10974

quotes.text: Sponsors shall review their directives annually to ensure they are necessary, current, and consistent with statutory authority. The results of the review shall be documented per the enclosure and retained according to reference (d).
Directives shall be reviewed annually to ensure that they are necessary; current; and consistent with Marine Corps policy, existing law, and statutory authority.
Upon completion of the review, the sponsors shall certify whether the directives shall be continued, revised, or cancelled.

Record the results of the review of your directives on NAVMC 10974 (Rev. 11/06), Directives Review card, including those that supplement a higher authority directive. NAVMC 10974 may be obtained on the Marine Corps Electronic Forms web site at http://www.hqmc.usmc.mil/ar/mcefs.nsf. Use the card until the last edition is canceled. Pencil in a new revision letter or change number and the new date. When the order cancels, move the card to a canceled file; check this file to avoid reassigning the consecutive point number. Commands may use an automated database file containing the same information as an option to recording the results of the review on a NAVMC 10974.

regulatory.summary: The command must conduct an annual review of all directives, document the results on NAVMC 10974 or an automated tracking system, and certify whether each directive is to be continued, revised, or cancelled.

actions: Ask the command to produce NAVMC 10974 forms or the automated tracking system records
Records required for every active directive
Reviews must be conducted annually
Check the date of the most recent review for each directive
Each review must evaluate whether the directive is:
Necessary
Current
Consistent with Marine Corps policy
Consistent with existing law
Consistent with statutory authority
Upon completion of the review, the sponsor must certify the directive as:
Continued, OR
Revised, OR
Cancelled
NAVMC 10974 (Rev. 11/06) is the standard form
Automated database file containing the same information is acceptable
Verify the format meets one of these two options
Pull the master directive listing
Verify every active directive has a corresponding NAVMC 10974 or tracking entry
Identify any directives missing from the review records
Review records must flag directives at or near the 9-year mark
Records must monitor target dates for completion or cancellation
Cross-reference with Q0103 findings
If any bulletins have Cancellation Contingency paragraphs, verify a NAVMC 10974 was prepared
Bulletin card attached to current order card until incorporated
No NAVMC 10974 or automated tracking system in use
Reviews not conducted annually
Review records missing for some active directives
Sponsor certification missing from review records
Review does not address necessity, currency, or statutory consistency
Aged directives not flagged in annual review
Cancellation Contingency bulletins without NAVMC 10974

questionsToAsk: Show me your NAVMC 10974 forms or automated tracking records for the past year?
Pull the master directive listing and let me cross-reference annual reviews?
For this directive, show me the most recent review and certification?
Who certifies whether a directive is continued, revised, or cancelled?
How do you track which directives are due for review each year?
verification: NAVMC 10974 forms or automated tracking system in use
Annual reviews conducted within previous 12 months
Every active directive has a review record
Reviews evaluate necessity, currency, and statutory consistency
